= 2I(X,W,Z;Y)-2I(Y;Z|X)   (since markov(W, Z, Y, X))

  = I(X;Y) + I(X;Y) + H(Y|X)                      (chain rule on H(Y))

  = H(W|Y,X)+I(X;Y,C_0)+I(C_0;W)+I(W;Y)-H(C_0|X)-I(W;X)   (since copy lemma: 2H(X,W) == 2H(X,C_0))
  = H(W|X)+H(W,X,Y)+I(C_0;W)+H(W,Y)-H(C_0|X)-H(C_0|Y)-H(Y|X)-H(C_0,X,Y)

  = I(W;Y|X)+I(C_0;W)+I(C_0;X;Y)+I(W,Y;X)-I(C_0;X,Y)   (since copy lemma: 3H(X,Y,C_0) == 3H(X,Y,W))

  <= I(W;Y|X)+H(C_0|X,Y)+I(W;Y|Z)+I(C_0,Z;X)+I(W,Y;X)-I(C_0;X|Y)-H(C_0|W,Z)

label: H(X|Y) >= H(X)
raw:
VERDICT: False
TYPE: N/A
Disproof:
  In general, conditioning cannot increase entropy: H(X|Y) <= H(X).
  Counterexample: let X be a fair bit and let Y=X. Then H(X)=1, but H(X|Y)=0.
  So H(X|Y) >= H(X) fails.

  Then Xâ”€Yâ”€Z holds because I(X;Z|Y) = 0.
  But I(X;Z) = 0 while I(X;Y) = H(X) = 1.
